heading: Pyth-2 page-110 promotion completed and page-75 lane started
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, pyth2, validated-workset, page110, page75]
body: Promoted the two clean Pyth-2 page-110 leftovers into the validated lane and kept the three convention-sensitive page-110 items isolated. `pyth2_validated_workset.json` now contains 5 stable items with counts `4 direct_validated / 1 corrected_source_validated`, namely fixed-`e=60` admissibility, reflection pairs about 30, the every-fifth Fibonacci divisibility rule for 5, the every-third Fibonacci divisibility rule for even terms, and the bounded coprime bead-pair counts `15/13/35`. The isolated page-110 convention issues are now in `pyth2_page110_issue_register.json` with 3 `source-layer ambiguity` holdouts: the source terms `3-tri` and `4-par`, plus the e-d count `18` that depends on including the unity root pair `(1,1)`. Expanded the stable Pyth-2 program/table layer so `pyth2_program_artifacts.json` now covers fixed-`e=60`, reflection pairs, Fibonacci periodicity for divisors 2 and 5, and bounded pair-count cases; `pyth2_validated_tables.json` now has 5 stable item rows, 16 admissibility rows, 8 reflection-pair rows, 10 periodicity rows, and 3 bounded-pair-count rows. Then turned back to the noisier page-75 prime-factor material by building `pyth2_page75_prime_factor_batch_001.json`, which starts a controlled batch of 4 items with one OCR-corruption holdout and three corrected-but-still-flagged source-layer ambiguities around the 5040/1000 prime-power wavelength decompositions.
